question_title: Will Cloudflare experience another critical incident before May 2026?
summary: The question asks whether Cloudflare will have a "critical" (red) incident on their status page between Feb 25 and May 1, 2026. I confirmed via the Statuspage.io JSON API that the Feb 4, 2026 Stream Live Playback incident was classified as "critical," establishing that Cloudflare does use this classification. However, the classification appears inconsistent — a 6-hour global BYOIP outage on Feb 20 was only "minor." With ~60 days remaining in the resolution window, a high overall incident volume (~100+/month), but rare use of the "critical" tag, I estimate ~57% probability using a Poisson mixture model weighted across plausible critical incident rates (1/month to 1/year). Code Orange provides modest downward pressure but hasn't prevented major outages.

factors.description: One confirmed critical incident (Feb 4, 2026) within the last 4 weeks establishes that Cloudflare actively uses the 'critical' classification on their status page
High incident volume (~100+ per month on status page) increases probability that at least one gets classified as 'critical' in 60 remaining days
Code Orange resilience plan in effect since Dec 2025, aimed at reducing incidents - but outages continue (Feb 20 6-hour outage happened post-Code Orange)

Classification appears highly inconsistent: 6-hour global outage (Feb 20) was 'minor', while limited-scope Stream issue (Feb 4) was 'critical' - suggests critical tag is idiosyncratic, not proportional to severity
No critical incidents in the first 5 days of the resolution window (Feb 25-Mar 2), mild evidence against very high rate
Cloudflare's infrastructure complexity and continuous deployment changes (root cause of most outages) ensure incidents will continue at high rate through the window
Community prediction at ~54% with 126 forecasters provides an independent anchor for moderate probability

reasoning:
**Forecast: 57% probability (logit: 0.3)**

Key reasoning:

- **Confirmed critical classification exists**: The Feb 4, 2026 Stream Live incident was verified as "critical" in the Statuspage.io JSON API, confirming Cloudflare does use this label.
- **But classification is inconsistent**: A massive 6-hour global BYOIP outage on Feb 20 was only classified as "minor," while the limited-scope Stream issue was "critical." This makes rate estimation difficult.
- **High incident volume**: Cloudflare has 100+ status page incidents per month, providing many opportunities for a "critical" tag.
- **Code Orange**: Cloudflare's post-Nov/Dec resilience plan provides modest downward pressure, but hasn't prevented major outages.
- **~60 days remaining**: The resolution window runs through May 1, with no critical incidents so far since Feb 25.
- **Poisson mixture model**: Weighting across plausible rates (1 critical/month to 1/year) gives ~49-57% depending on Code Orange assumptions.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Cloudflare experience another critical incident before May 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42302)
[(Metaculus) Will Cloudflare experience another critical incident before May 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42284)
resolution_criteria:
This question will resolve as Yes if after February 25, 2026 and before May 1, 2026, Cloudflare experiences a critical incident.

The primary source for this question will be the [Cloudflare Status Page](https://www.cloudflarestatus.com/history), with critical incidents shown in red.
fine_print: This question will only consider reporting and categorisation directly from Cloudflare. If Cloudflare changes their categorisation scheme such that no comparable category exists, this question will be annulled.
